title: Frost Family Deep Ancestry Research
task: Push the Frost line as far back as possible from Thomas Claiborne Frost I (1833-1903), founder of Frost Bank, San Antonio TX. Also document the Herff and Kampmann families connected to the Frost network.
methodology_note: Sources consulted: TSHA Handbook of Texas Online (primary), Wikipedia (Ferdinand Ludwig Herff), Internet Archive genealogy texts, Geni.com family tree data, Encyclopedia.com business history, San Antonio Report, Boerne Book, WikiTree surname index, Washington & Lee obituary, FundingUniverse corporate history. Find a Grave and Ancestry returned 403/empty. FamilySearch returned browser-incompatible response. Tavily hit rate limit. All confidence markings are conservative.
summary: Thomas Claiborne Frost I (1833-1903) arrived in Texas in 1854 from Belle Font, Jackson County, Alabama. His parents were Thomas C. Frost and Crissy (Price) Frost. Earlier generations have not been independently verified in accessible primary sources. The Frost surname is Anglo-Saxon in origin (Old English 'frost'), well-documented in England from 1086 and in colonial America from 1630, reaching the Southern US via New Jersey → North Carolina → Tennessee → Alabama migration patterns documented for related Frost lines. There is no evidence of Frost presence in Texas during the Spanish colonial or Republic of Texas era (pre-1854). The Herff family (German-Hessian aristocracy) and Kampmann family (Prussian craftsmen) are the key collateral San Antonio families connected to the Frost banking dynasty by marriage, not by direct blood relationship to Hunter Spence's confirmed maternal line (Lepick/Boehme).

critical_context.note: Hunter Spence's confirmed maternal line is Lepick, NOT Frost. Shari Mattingly's mother was Jennive Imogene Lepick (1923-2008), daughter of Frederick Charles Lepick Sr. and Hilda Boehme Lepick. The Frost/Herff family network is historically documented and interesting as San Antonio notable families but has NO confirmed connection to Hunter's direct ancestry. These findings document the Frost line for collateral/context purposes only.

confirmed_ancestors.name: Thomas Claiborne Frost I
confirmed_ancestors.birth_place: Belle Font, Jackson County, Alabama, USA
confirmed_ancestors.death_place: San Antonio, Bexar County, Texas, USA
confirmed_ancestors.fact: Founder of Frost National Bank (San Antonio, chartered 1899). Arrived in Texas 1854. Graduated Irving College, Tennessee, 1853. Read law under Sam Houston and Henderson King Yoakum. Admitted to bar ~1856. Civil War service as Lt. Colonel, First Mounted Rifles. Founded mercantile firm Fitch, Frost and Brother 1868.
confirmed_ancestors.spouse: 1st: Mrs. Betty Eastland Roberts (married 30 Nov 1858, Belton TX; one daughter survived to adulthood); 2nd: Josephine Houston (married 31 Dec 1878; four children)
confirmed_ancestors.parents: Thomas C. Frost (father) and Crissy (Price) Frost (mother)

confirmed_ancestors.name: Josephine Houston Frost
confirmed_ancestors.fact: Second wife of Thomas Claiborne Frost I. Geni profile confirmed her birth/death years and marriage. Mother of: Lucy Fannie Frost (1888-1976, married Ferdinand P. Herff), Thomas Clayborne Frost II, Joseph Hardin Frost, Josephine Frost, and John M. Frost.
sources: TSHA Handbook — lists four children from second marriage

confirmed_ancestors.name: Thomas Clayborne Frost II
confirmed_ancestors.fact: Son of Thomas Claiborne Frost I and Josephine Houston. Succeeded his father as Frost Bank president upon founder's death in 1903; served until 1926 when he became chairman. His brother Joseph H. Frost then became president.

confirmed_ancestors.name: Joseph Hardin Frost
confirmed_ancestors.fact: Son of Thomas Claiborne Frost I and Josephine Houston. Bank president 1926 onward. Married Eda Kampmann (a granddaughter of John Herman Kampmann, the San Antonio builder — NOTE: this Eda Kampmann is distinct from Eda Caroline Kampmann Meyer, 1855-1925). Find a Grave memorial 22626951.

confirmed_ancestors.name: Thomas Clayborne Frost III
confirmed_ancestors.fact: Son of Thomas Clayborne Frost II and Lillie Clark Beall (his mother's maiden name per strongfamilytree.org). Known as 'T.C. Frost Sr.' in his son's era. Married Florence Ilse Herff (1906-1984) on 3 Aug 1925.
confirmed_ancestors.spouse: Florence Ilse Herff Frost (1906-1984; married 3 Aug 1925)
confirmed_ancestors.parents: Thomas Clayborne Frost II (father) and Lillie Clark Beall (mother)
sources: Find a Grave data cited in prior research (research/19-frost-maternal.json)

confirmed_ancestors.name: Thomas Clayborne Frost IV (Tom Frost Jr.)
confirmed_ancestors.fact: Son of Thomas Clayborne Frost III and Florence Ilse Herff. Graduated Washington & Lee University 1950. President of Culberson-Frost Bankers (Frost Bank) 1963-1997; Chairman Emeritus until death. Married Patricia 'Pat' Holden Frost; four children: Tom III, Bill H., Don B., Pat B. Find a Grave 192133735.

probable_ancestors.name: Thomas C. Frost (father of founder)
probable_ancestors.birth_place: Unknown — possibly Tennessee or Virginia based on Alabama migration patterns of the era
probable_ancestors.fact: Named in TSHA as father of Thomas Claiborne Frost I. First name Thomas and surname Frost confirmed. No birth/death dates found in accessible sources. Wife: Crissy (Price) Frost. Belle Font, Jackson County, Alabama became the county seat in 1821, suggesting the family arrived in Alabama in the early territorial period (~1820s-1833). The 1830 or 1840 Jackson County census would likely contain a Thomas Frost household.
sources: TSHA Handbook of Texas (names parents of T.C. Frost I)

probable_ancestors.name: Crissy (Price) Frost
probable_ancestors.birth_place: Unknown — possibly Virginia, North Carolina, or Tennessee
probable_ancestors.fact: Named in TSHA as mother of Thomas Claiborne Frost I. Maiden name Price confirmed. Price is a Welsh/English surname common in Virginia and the Carolinas during this period. No further genealogical detail found in accessible sources.
sources: TSHA Handbook of Texas (names parents of T.C. Frost I)

probable_ancestors.name: Lucy Fannie Frost Herff
probable_ancestors.fact: Daughter of Thomas Claiborne Frost I and Josephine Houston. Married Ferdinand P. Herff (son of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff's line), creating a second Frost-Herff marriage in the San Antonio notable-family network. Geni profile confirmed.

herff_family.note: The Herff family is documented here because of direct connections to the Frost family through two marriages: (1) Lucy Fannie Frost married Ferdinand P. Herff; (2) Thomas Clayborne Frost III married Florence Ilse Herff. The Herff family is NOT part of Hunter Spence's confirmed maternal line.

confirmed_members.name: Ferdinand Ludwig von Herff (later Ferdinand Ludwig Herff)
confirmed_members.birth_place: Darmstadt, Grand Duchy of Hesse, Germany
confirmed_members.fact: Pioneer physician of Texas. Participated in the Bettina commune in Texas 1847. Returned to Germany then came back permanently, settling in New Braunfels then San Antonio 1850. Dropped the aristocratic 'von' upon becoming an American citizen. Founded San Antonio's early medical institution. Had six children. His father was Christian von Herff (Privy Councillor / Supreme Court Judge, Hesse-Darmstadt) and his mother was Eleanora von Meusebach (cousin of John O. Meusebach, the German colonizer of Fredericksburg TX). Buried: San Antonio City Cemetery No. 1. Find a Grave 14088285.
confirmed_members.parents: Christian von Herff (father) and Eleanora von Meusebach (mother)

confirmed_members.name: Christian von Herff
confirmed_members.fact: Father of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff. Privy Councillor and Judge of the Hessian Supreme Court. Married Eleanora von Meusebach.
sources: Wikipedia article on Ferdinand Ludwig Herff

confirmed_members.name: Eleanora von Meusebach (Herff)
confirmed_members.fact: Mother of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff. A cousin of John O. Meusebach (born Ottfried Hans Freiherr von Meusebach, 1812-1897), the German colonizer who founded Fredericksburg, Texas and negotiated the Meusebach-Comanche Treaty of 1847. John Meusebach's father was Baron Karl Hartwig Gregor von Meusebach; Eleanora was from the same aristocratic Meusebach family of Hesse.
sources: Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ferdinand_Ludwig_Herff (Eleanora named as cousin of John O. Meusebach)

confirmed_members.name: John Bennett Herff (Dr.)
confirmed_members.fact: Son of Eda Caroline Kampmann and her first husband Dr. John Herff (a son of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff). Grandson of both Ferdinand Ludwig Herff and John Herman Kampmann. Physician in San Antonio. Married Florence Harris in 1902. Children: Carol (1904-1922), Florence Ilse (1906-1984), Jean (1910-1996). Find a Grave 94207142.
confirmed_members.parents: Eda Caroline Kampmann (mother) and Dr. John Herff (father, a son of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff)
sources: John Benedict article on Caleb's Log Cabin (cited in research/47-frost-herff-san-antonio.md)

confirmed_members.name: Florence Ilse Herff Frost
confirmed_members.fact: Daughter of Dr. John Bennett Herff and Florence Harris. Granddaughter of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff (via John Bennett Herff) and John Herman Kampmann (via Eda Kampmann). Married Thomas Clayborne Frost III on 3 Aug 1925. Mother of Tom Frost Jr. (1927-2018) and Ilse 'Ita' Herff Frost McNeel (1930-2007). Find a Grave 22626925.
sources: Find a Grave 22626925 (cited in prior research)

kampmann_family.note: John Herman Kampmann was a founding figure of San Antonio. His daughter Eda Caroline Kampmann (1855-1925) married twice: first to Dr. John Herff (son of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff), then to J.H. Frost (a relative of the bank Frost family). This created the Kampmann-Herff-Frost network of San Antonio elite families.

confirmed_members.name: John Herman Kampmann
confirmed_members.death_place: Colorado Springs, Colorado (body returned to San Antonio for burial)
confirmed_members.fact: Master builder of San Antonio. Left Europe 1848, settled in San Antonio. Married Caroline Bonnet (also Prussian) in 1850. Built the Menger Hotel (1858-1859) and much of early San Antonio — credited with building one-third of the city. Attended University of Cologne before emigrating. Children: Gustav (son), Herman (son), Eda (daughter), Eliza (daughter, died young), Lina (daughter, died young). Find a Grave 15903766.
confirmed_members.parents: John Peter Kampmann (father) and Elizabeth Finniman Selingshof Kampmann (mother)

confirmed_members.name: John Peter Kampmann
confirmed_members.fact: Father of John Herman Kampmann. Named in TSHA Handbook as parent of the San Antonio builder.

confirmed_members.name: Eda Caroline Kampmann (Meyer / Frost)
confirmed_members.fact: Daughter of John Herman Kampmann and Caroline Bonnet. First married Dr. John Herff (a son of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff) — he died ~1882 at age 31. Their son: John Bennett Herff (1878-1959). After Dr. Herff's death she remarried. Also connected to J.H. Frost (a San Antonio Fiesta court record places her as 'queen' to J.H. Frost's 'prime minister'). Find a Grave 15903818 lists her as 'Eda Caroline Kampmann Meyer' — the Meyer may refer to a second or third marriage record.
confirmed_members.spouse: 1st: Dr. John Herff (son of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff); 2nd: possibly J.H. Frost or Dr. Meyer

frost_surname_origin.derivation: From the Old English word 'frost' or 'forst' (ice crystals, freezing weather). Used as a nickname for someone with white or gray hair, a cold demeanor, or born during a harsh winter. Not Germanic in the Frost bank family's case — the name predates German immigration patterns.
frost_surname_origin.earliest_english_record: William Forst, Domesday Book of Hampshire, 1086
other_early_records: Lefstan Frost, Feudal Documents of Bury St. Edmunds, Suffolk, 1095
frost_surname_origin.colonial_america_arrival: Nicholas Frost and William Frost arrived in America 1630. Thomas Frost (age 28) sailed from London on the ship 'John' in October 1635.
frost_surname_origin.american_migration_pattern: New England / Mid-Atlantic colonies → New Jersey → North Carolina → Tennessee → Alabama → Texas. This matches the pattern documented for the Bedford County, Tennessee Frost family (Ebenezer Frost Sr., b. NJ 1746, settled NC then TN). Thomas Claiborne Frost's Alabama origin fits this geographic progression.
sources: archive.org: 'The Frosts and Related Families of Bedford County, Tennessee'

republic_of_texas_era_assessment.finding: No Frost presence confirmed in Texas during the Spanish colonial era (pre-1836) or Republic of Texas era (1836-1845). Thomas Claiborne Frost I arrived in Texas in 1854, well after Texas statehood (1845). No Frost land grants or early settler records found for Bexar County from the Spanish or Republic period.
republic_of_texas_era_assessment.note: The TSHA states he 'came to Texas from Alabama in 1855' (one source says 1854, another 1855 — likely arrived 1854, began work at Austin College which opened fall 1854).

two_eda_kampmanns_clarification.note: There is a critical distinction in the Frost-Kampmann genealogy that prior research files may have conflated.

eda_1.name: Eda Caroline Kampmann Meyer
eda_1.relationship: Daughter of John Herman Kampmann (the San Antonio builder). First married Dr. John Herff (son of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff). Their son: Dr. John Bennett Herff (1878-1959). Second marriage unclear (Meyer or Frost).
eda_1.connection_to_frost_bank: Collateral — her son John Bennett Herff married Florence Harris; their daughter Florence Ilse Herff married T.C. Frost III.

eda_2.name: Eda Kampmann Frost (wife of Joseph Hardin Frost, 1881-1956)
eda_2.relationship: A different Eda Kampmann — likely a granddaughter or niece of John Herman Kampmann, not his daughter (generational math: Eda 1 was born 1855, same generation as Joseph H. Frost's parents). Details not fully confirmed in accessible sources.
eda_2.connection_to_frost_bank: Direct — married Joseph Hardin Frost, the Frost Bank president 1926-1956.

corrections.correction: Thomas Clayborne Frost III's mother was NOT Eda Kampmann. His mother was Lillie Clark Beall (per strongfamilytree.org genealogy). T.C. Frost III was a grandson of the founder through the T.C. Frost II line, not the Joseph H. Frost / Eda Kampmann line.

Florence Ilse Herff Frost (wife of T.C. Frost III) was a granddaughter of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff — NOT a daughter. Her father was Dr. John Bennett Herff (1878-1959), who was the son of Eda Caroline Kampmann and her first husband Dr. John Herff (a son of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff).
corrections.source: John Benedict article cited in research/47-frost-herff-san-antonio.md

corrections.correction: The Frost-Kampmann connection entered the family through TWO separate marriages, not one: (1) Lucy Fannie Frost (daughter of the founder) married Ferdinand P. Herff; (2) T.C. Frost III (grandson of the founder) married Florence Ilse Herff (a Kampmann-Herff granddaughter). Additionally, Joseph Hardin Frost (another son of the founder) married an Eda Kampmann.
corrections.source: Geni profile for Lucie Herff; San Antonio Report article; prior research files

corrections.correction: Shari Mattingly's maternal line is confirmed as Lepick, NOT Frost. The oral family tradition of a 'Frost Bank uncle' is unverified mythology. The Frost/Herff/Kampmann research in files 19, 47 is collateral context only.

next_steps.action: Obtain 'Character Endures: The Genealogy of Thomas Claiborne Frost' by David Haynes (2002, ISBN 9780971991705). This 155-page book was commissioned by the Frost family and almost certainly contains the full lineage back to Alabama, Tennessee, and England including Thomas C. Frost and Crissy (Price) Frost and their antecedents.
next_steps.where: Amazon, AbeBooks, or San Antonio Public Library / Bexar County Genealogical Society

next_steps.action: Search 1830 and 1840 Federal Census, Jackson County, Alabama for a Thomas Frost household with wife 'Crissy' or 'Chrissie' (maiden Price). This would identify birth state origins via census birthplace data and establish whether children (including TC Frost I's birth in 1833) appear.
next_steps.where: Ancestry.com or FamilySearch (Jackson County, Alabama census collections)

next_steps.action: Search Chancery Court records for Jackson County, Alabama (Scottsboro) for any Frost probate, land, or deed records 1821-1860 that would name Thomas C. Frost Sr. as grantor/grantee and identify his origin state.
next_steps.where: Alabama Department of Archives and History (ADAH) or Jackson County Probate Court

next_steps.action: Verify Eda Kampmann (wife of Joseph Hardin Frost) — confirm whether she was a granddaughter of John Herman Kampmann (making her the daughter of Gustav or Herman Kampmann) or a more distant relation. The generational timing suggests granddaughter, not daughter.
next_steps.where: Find a Grave search for 'Eda Kampmann Frost'; Texas Birth Index 1875-1895 for Bexar County

next_steps.action: Confirm Ferdinand P. Herff (who married Lucy Fannie Frost in ~1908-1910) and his place in the Herff family tree — which of Ferdinand Ludwig Herff's six children was his father?
next_steps.where: UTSA Libraries Special Collections — Herff family papers; 'The Doctors Herff: A Three Generation Memoir'

next_steps.action: Search the Price surname in Jackson County, Alabama records 1820-1850 to identify Crissy (Price) Frost's parents. Price families were common in Virginia and the Carolinas; tracing her family would potentially extend the maternal line of Thomas Claiborne Frost I further back.
next_steps.where: FamilySearch: Jackson County Alabama records; Virginia Price families 1780-1820

next_steps.action: Check the Frost DNA surname project at FamilyTreeDNA for haplogroup data matching Belle Font / Jackson County Alabama Frost families. May confirm or deny connection to the New Jersey / North Carolina Frost lines documented in 'The Frosts of Bedford County, Tennessee.'
